citation: Li, Ziying and Hanjung Lee. 2021. Syntactic Distribution of the Semantic Classes of Dative Verbs in English and Cantonese: A Crosslinguistic Perspective. PACLIC 35: 628-639.
claim_supported: Three major dative orders; lexical bei2 special status; communication predicates associated with V-Recipient-Theme; semantic classes of pure caused possession, transfer possession, and caused motion; PDC/DOC meaning differences; heavy/focused Theme condition for double-bei2 profile.
limitations: Author judgments and a theory-driven semantic account do not establish current corpus frequency, exhaustive lexical membership, regional uniformity, or exact parser nodes. Communicative predicates are explicitly set aside rather than fully analyzed.

citation: Xu, Liejiong and Alain Peyraube. 1997. On the Double Object Construction and the Oblique Construction in Cantonese. Studies in Language 21(1):105-127.
claim_supported: Theme-Recipient is predominant; Recipient-Theme is not a free variant; lexical, emphatic, semantic-role, weight, and information-structure restrictions interact; Theme-Recipient shares restrictions with Theme-marker-Recipient.
limitations: 1990s judgments and the proposed preposition-deletion derivation are contested. Predominance does not imply unrestricted modern productivity.

citation: Tong, Angel Man-Shan and Kwing Lok Albert Lee. 2024. An acceptability study of triadic constructions in Hong Kong Cantonese. Lingua 307:103749.
locator: https://hub.hku.hk/handle/10722/358607; abstract; see UC-RQ-002 full-text extraction
claim_supported: Non-bei2 markerless Theme-Recipient is prosodically, weight-, pause-, age-, and prominence-conditioned; a written-order rule is insufficient.
limitations: Detailed statistics and the internally inconsistent pause-direction report are maintained in UC-RQ-002, not repeated as settled facts here.

citation: Chin, Andy Chi-on. 2022. Cantonese GIVE and Double-Object Construction: Grammaticalization and Word Order Change. John Benjamins.
claim_supported: The book treats Northern/Southern order typology, relation between IO-DO and DO-IO, and ongoing contact-related word-order change using historical, corpus, production, perception, and a 40-speaker sociolinguistic survey.
limitations: Full chapter text was not accessible in this pass. No numerical result, detailed conditioning factor, or conclusion beyond the official overview is imported.

citation: Canto Span CP021B source screen and source register.
claim_supported: Existing project already records exact sources for lexical GIVE, post-theme bei2, marker-category disputes, and naturalness conflicts, preventing rediscovery from being misrepresented as new evidence.
limitations: Project synthesis is not independent external evidence and does not count toward linguistic support by itself.

citation: Luke & Wong 2015. The Hong Kong Cantonese Corpus: Design and Uses.
locator: official fcbond/hkcancor UTF-8 release at commit 39aeadf920e0b5ca93d0ad7792c59e740e7bdd65; UC-RQ-002-003-DOUBLE-OBJECT-HKCANCOR-CENSUS-R1.md
claim_supported: A manual census of 18 送, 26 寄, and 51 教 verb tokens naturally attests instruction V-Recipient-Theme/content and overt-marker transfer; it also supplies a false-positive Theme-plus-purpose-clause diagnostic.
limitations: Lexically narrow transcript evidence cannot resolve non-畀 IDOC prosody, prove corpus-wide frequency, or establish a negative grammar boundary.
